summary: DHH shares a quote from John Robb announcing his switch from PC to Apple after 18 years, using it to illustrate the growing momentum of people abandoning Windows for Mac. He notes that even his girlfriend, a former Mac critic, is warming up to Apple thanks to Safari's SnapBack feature and the iPod. DHH himself is eyeing the new 12-inch PowerBook and trying to sell off his Sony Vaio.
key_points: Windows fatigue was driving tech-savvy users to switch to Mac in the early 2000s
Apple's ecosystem approach (iPod, Safari features) was converting even skeptics
DHH was an early Mac evangelist, already on an iBook and upgrading to a PowerBook
money_quotes: Even my girl friend, who used to bash the Mac, is getting there.
Now I just need to push that Sony Vaio out the door. Any staunch defenders of the PC interested? It's cheap!
key_insight: Apple's combination of polished software and hardware ecosystem was creating a wave of PC-to-Mac converts in the early 2000s, even among longtime skeptics.
